text.title: Information Architecture — Templates Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Information Architecture — Templates. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every IA deliverable includes the four canonical artefacts — sitemap, taxonomy document, navigation specification, and page inventory — even if some are short.
rule.rationale: Missing artefacts cause downstream contributors to invent their own format.

rule.statement: The page-inventory template requires an owner column with a real human handle on every row; 'team' or blank is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without an owner pages drift out of date and become orphan content.

rule.statement: Every artefact carries a version, last_reviewed date, and link back to the framework that generated it.
rule.rationale: Artefacts without provenance cannot be updated confidently by later contributors.

rule.statement: The taxonomy document is exported as both human-readable Markdown and machine-readable YAML so CMS or search systems can consume it.
rule.rationale: Single-format taxonomies cannot be enforced by automation.

rule.statement: The navigation specification names the exact component variant from the design system for each navigation type.
rule.rationale: Navigation that drifts from the design system breaks visual hierarchy guarantees.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-ia-templates.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
